The post CNN London Internships 2026: Apply for Multiple Journalism, Sports, Video Editing, and Newsroom Opportunities in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The global media industry continues to offer exceptional opportunities for aspiring journalists and media professionals, and CNN has once again opened several exciting internship and apprenticeship positions in London, United Kingdom. These opportunities provide aspiring media professionals with direct exposure to one of the world’s most influential news organizations.

For students, recent graduates, and early-career professionals looking to launch a career in journalism, broadcasting, digital media, sports reporting, content creation, video production, or newsroom operations, CNN’s 2026 London internship opportunities represent an invaluable stepping stone toward professional success.

Through hands-on newsroom experience, mentorship from industry experts, and exposure to international news coverage, successful applicants will gain practical skills that are highly sought after across the global media landscape.

Why CNN Internships Matter

CNN is recognized worldwide for delivering breaking news, in-depth reporting, investigative journalism, and international coverage across television, digital platforms, and social media.

Interning at CNN allows participants to:

  • Work alongside experienced journalists and producers.
  • Gain exposure to international news operations.
  • Develop professional reporting and storytelling skills.
  • Learn newsroom workflows and editorial processes.
  • Build a professional network within the media industry.
  • Gain practical experience that strengthens future job applications.
  • Understand multimedia journalism across TV, digital, and social platforms.

These internships provide a rare opportunity to learn directly from professionals operating at the highest levels of global journalism.

Available CNN London Internship and Apprenticeship Opportunities

CNN is currently offering the following early-career opportunities in London:

1. CNN London Sports Intern

Location: London, United Kingdom

CNN International’s flagship sports program, World Sport, airs multiple times daily throughout the week.

The Sports Intern will contribute to:

  • Sports news coverage
  • Research activities
  • High-profile athlete interviews
  • Story development
  • Production support
  • Coverage of major sporting events
  • Exploration of important themes such as racism, diversity, inclusion, and gender issues in sports

This role is ideal for candidates passionate about sports journalism and international sports coverage.

2. CNN News Desk Reporting Intern

Location: London, United Kingdom

CNN’s award-winning London News Desk is seeking aspiring journalists interested in international and breaking news.

Responsibilities may include:

  • News gathering
  • Research and verification
  • Reporting assistance
  • Monitoring global news developments
  • Supporting newsroom operations
  • Assisting journalists covering breaking stories

This internship offers exposure to real-time news production and international reporting.

3. CNNIC Content Sales & Strategic Partnerships Apprentice

Location: London, United Kingdom

This apprenticeship combines business development, content strategy, and client engagement.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Researching consumer and client needs
  • Supporting content sales initiatives
  • Building client relationships
  • Identifying new partnership opportunities
  • Working with internal teams
  • Supporting strategic growth initiatives

This role is suitable for candidates interested in media business, marketing, partnerships, and content commercialization.

4. CNN London Planning (Interviews & Guest Booking) Intern

Location: London, United Kingdom

The Planning Desk sits at the center of CNN’s newsroom operations.

Responsibilities include:

  • Identifying compelling interview opportunities
  • Booking guests for CNN programs
  • Supporting breaking news coverage
  • Researching experts and commentators
  • Coordinating interviews
  • Assisting editorial planning teams

Candidates interested in newsroom coordination and editorial planning will find this role particularly valuable.

5. CNN London Features Video Editor Intern

Location: London, United Kingdom

CNN International Productions develops feature content for television, digital platforms, and social media.

Interns will:

  • Assist editors with production workflows
  • Prepare video edits
  • Create feature content
  • Support television and digital productions
  • Work on short-form storytelling projects
  • Learn professional editing techniques

This opportunity is excellent for aspiring video editors and multimedia storytellers.

6. CNN London News Desk Video Intern

Location: London, United Kingdom

The Video Newsdesk plays a central role in CNN’s global storytelling operations.

Key duties include:

  • Producing video content
  • Supporting digital and broadcast teams
  • Managing multiple projects simultaneously
  • Creating high-quality visual news content
  • Assisting breaking news coverage
  • Supporting newsroom video operations

Candidates with strong multimedia and video production interests are encouraged to apply.

7. CNN London Features (TV & Digital) Intern

Location: London, United Kingdom

This internship supports CNN International Productions across television and digital content.

Intern responsibilities may include:

  • Story research
  • Editorial planning
  • Script writing
  • Production assistance
  • Content development
  • Supporting television and digital storytelling initiatives

The role provides valuable experience in long-form feature production and multimedia journalism.

Benefits of Joining CNN’s Internship Program

Successful interns may benefit from:

Professional Development

  • Real-world newsroom experience
  • Exposure to international journalism standards
  • Mentorship from experienced professionals
  • Skill development in reporting, production, and storytelling

Career Advancement

  • Enhanced CV and portfolio
  • Networking opportunities
  • Industry exposure
  • Improved employability within global media organizations

Practical Experience

  • Participation in live newsroom environments
  • Exposure to breaking news operations
  • Multimedia production experience
  • Hands-on project involvement

Skills Candidates Can Develop

Participants can strengthen competencies in:

  • Journalism
  • News reporting
  • Broadcast production
  • Video editing
  • Script writing
  • Story development
  • Research and verification
  • Interview coordination
  • Digital storytelling
  • Social media content production
  • Strategic communication
  • Partnership management

How to Apply

Interested candidates should:

  1. Visit the official Warner Bros. Discovery Early Careers portal.
  2. Search for the desired internship or apprenticeship position.
  3. Review eligibility requirements carefully.
  4. Prepare an updated CV and supporting documents.
  5. Submit the application before positions are filled.

Because internship opportunities at major international media organizations attract significant competition, candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

Application Deadline

No specific application deadline has been announced.

Applications are likely to remain open until positions are filled. Early submission is strongly recommended.

Final Thoughts

CNN’s 2026 London internship opportunities provide a unique pathway into the world of international journalism, sports media, television production, digital storytelling, and newsroom operations. Whether your ambition is to become a reporter, producer, editor, sports journalist, content strategist, or media executive, these internships offer hands-on experience within one of the world’s most respected news organizations.

For aspiring media professionals seeking meaningful exposure to global journalism and broadcasting, these opportunities could be the ideal launchpad for a successful career.

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Internship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post CNN London Internships 2026: Apply for Multiple Journalism, Sports, Video Editing, and Newsroom Opportunities in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNICEF Kenya and Standard Chartered Futuremakers Launch Grant Opportunity to Support Young Women’s Employment Through the Yoma Digital Ecosystem
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Organizations working to improve youth employment, digital inclusion, and women’s economic empowerment now have an exciting opportunity to partner with UNICEF Kenya and Standard Chartered Futuremakers through a new Request for Proposal (RFP) published on the United Nations Global Marketplace (UNGM). The funding opportunity, titled “Request for Proposal for Links to Work Programme — Supporting […]

The post UNICEF Kenya and Standard Chartered Futuremakers Launch Grant Opportunity to Support Young Women’s Employment Through the Yoma Digital Ecosystem appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations working to improve youth employment, digital inclusion, and women’s economic empowerment now have an exciting opportunity to partner with UNICEF Kenya and Standard Chartered Futuremakers through a new Request for Proposal (RFP) published on the United Nations Global Marketplace (UNGM).

The funding opportunity, titled “Request for Proposal for Links to Work Programme — Supporting Young Women Aged 18–24 from Underserved Backgrounds to Access Sustainable Employment Through the Yoma Digital Ecosystem in Partnership with UNICEF Kenya and Standard Chartered Futuremakers,” seeks qualified organizations to implement initiatives that help young women build employability skills and transition into sustainable employment opportunities.

This initiative reflects a shared commitment to expanding access to decent work, digital opportunities, and economic empowerment for underserved young women in Kenya through innovative partnerships and technology-driven solutions.

About the Opportunity

The Links to Work Programme is designed to strengthen employment pathways for young women by leveraging the Yoma digital ecosystem, an innovative platform that connects young people with learning opportunities, skills development, work experiences, and employment opportunities.

Through this Request for Proposal, UNICEF Kenya and Standard Chartered Futuremakers are seeking capable implementation partners that can support young women in overcoming barriers to employment while strengthening their digital and professional skills.

The programme specifically targets young women aged 18 to 24 years from underserved communities, recognizing that many continue to face significant social, economic, and structural challenges in accessing quality employment opportunities.

Programme Objectives

The selected implementing organization(s) will contribute to advancing youth employment by supporting activities that help participants prepare for and secure sustainable employment.

The programme aims to:

  • Improve employability among underserved young women.
  • Expand access to digital opportunities through the Yoma platform.
  • Strengthen workforce readiness.
  • Promote sustainable employment outcomes.
  • Support women’s economic empowerment.
  • Increase access to skills development opportunities.
  • Foster partnerships that connect young women to employers and employment ecosystems.

Focus on the Yoma Digital Ecosystem

The initiative places significant emphasis on the Yoma digital ecosystem, which serves as a platform connecting young people with opportunities that improve their employment prospects.

Through Yoma, participants may benefit from opportunities such as:

  • Digital learning.
  • Skills development.
  • Career readiness activities.
  • Employment pathways.
  • Youth engagement opportunities.
  • Connections to employers and ecosystem partners.

By integrating participants into this digital ecosystem, the programme seeks to create long-term employment outcomes rather than short-term interventions.

Who Should Apply?

Organizations with strong experience in youth employment, workforce development, digital skills training, and women’s empowerment are encouraged to submit proposals.

Potential applicants include:

  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • Civil society organizations
  • Youth development organizations
  • Women’s empowerment organizations
  • Workforce development agencies
  • Registered training institutions
  • Social enterprises
  • Private sector organizations
  • Partnerships and consortia

Organizations should possess the technical expertise and operational capacity required to implement high-quality employment programmes targeting underserved young women.

Expected Areas of Expertise

Successful applicants should demonstrate experience in areas such as:

  • Youth employment programming
  • Employability training
  • Career development
  • Digital skills training
  • Entrepreneurship support
  • Women’s economic empowerment
  • Community outreach
  • Workforce development
  • Monitoring and evaluation
  • Partnership management

Benefits of Participating

Organizations selected through this Request for Proposal may benefit from:

  • Partnership with UNICEF Kenya.
  • Collaboration with Standard Chartered Futuremakers.
  • Opportunity to contribute to national youth employment goals.
  • Increased organizational visibility.
  • Strengthened institutional partnerships.
  • Opportunity to improve employment outcomes for underserved young women.
  • Participation in an innovative digital employment ecosystem.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Youth unemployment remains one of the most pressing development challenges affecting many countries, including Kenya. Young women often experience additional barriers due to limited access to education, technology, employment networks, and financial resources.

By investing in innovative employment pathways through the Yoma digital ecosystem, this programme seeks to close opportunity gaps while supporting inclusive economic growth and sustainable livelihoods.

The initiative also aligns with broader efforts to advance gender equality, digital inclusion, and decent work for young people.

Application Process

Interested organizations should carefully review the complete Request for Proposal published on the United Nations Global Marketplace (UNGM).

Applicants should ensure that they understand all submission requirements, eligibility criteria, technical specifications, and proposal guidelines before preparing their applications.

Organizations are encouraged to prepare strong technical proposals that clearly demonstrate:

  • Organizational capacity.
  • Relevant implementation experience.
  • Technical expertise.
  • Understanding of youth employment challenges.
  • Ability to deliver measurable employment outcomes.
  • Strong monitoring and reporting systems.

Application Deadline

No application deadline has been provided in the available announcement.

Interested organizations should visit the official UNGM procurement opportunity page and submit their proposals as early as possible once all required documentation has been prepared.

How to Apply

Applications should be submitted through the United Nations Global Marketplace (UNGM) procurement platform.

Applicants should access the official procurement notice, review all proposal requirements, and follow the submission instructions provided in the Request for Proposal documentation before submitting their applications.

Apply Here

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post UNICEF Kenya and Standard Chartered Futuremakers Launch Grant Opportunity to Support Young Women’s Employment Through the Yoma Digital Ecosystem appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Policy & Advocacy Fellow at Anthropocene Alliance (A2): Fully Remote U.S. Environmental Justice Job Paying $65,000 Per Year
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The Anthropocene Alliance (A2), the largest coalition of frontline communities fighting for environmental justice across the United States and its territories, is currently accepting applications for a full-time Policy & Advocacy Fellow. This exciting fully remote opportunity is designed for professionals who are passionate about public policy, grassroots advocacy, climate justice, and community empowerment. Offering […]

The post Policy & Advocacy Fellow at Anthropocene Alliance (A2): Fully Remote U.S. Environmental Justice Job Paying $65,000 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Anthropocene Alliance (A2), the largest coalition of frontline communities fighting for environmental justice across the United States and its territories, is currently accepting applications for a full-time Policy & Advocacy Fellow. This exciting fully remote opportunity is designed for professionals who are passionate about public policy, grassroots advocacy, climate justice, and community empowerment.

Offering an annual salary of $65,000 plus benefits, this position provides an exceptional opportunity to work alongside one of the nation’s leading environmental justice organizations while helping frontline communities transform local environmental challenges into coordinated policy action.

If you have experience in public policy, advocacy, community organizing, or environmental justice and are looking to make a meaningful impact at a national level, this could be the ideal opportunity.

About Anthropocene Alliance (A2)

Anthropocene Alliance (A2) is the largest coalition of frontline communities in the United States dedicated to environmental protection and environmental justice.

The organization currently represents more than 430 member organizations working across the United States and its territories. These grassroots groups are directly affected by climate change, pollution, flooding, mining, environmental degradation, and other environmental challenges.

Rather than working independently, A2 helps unite these organizations, strengthens their collective voice, and supports coordinated advocacy efforts aimed at influencing environmental policies and promoting long-term systems change.

About the Policy & Advocacy Fellow Position

The Policy & Advocacy Fellow will play a central role in strengthening the policy capacity of frontline communities.

Working closely with member organizations, the successful candidate will help transform local environmental concerns into coordinated advocacy campaigns that can influence policy at state and local levels.

This is a full-time position (40 hours per week) and is completely remote, allowing qualified applicants based anywhere in the United States to apply.

The appointment is initially for two years, with the possibility of becoming a permanent position depending on future funding.

The role is funded through the newly established Strengthening Human Infrastructure Project (SHIP) at the Aspen Global Change Institute, where Anthropocene Alliance serves as one of the core partners.

Salary and Benefits

Successful applicants will receive:

  • Annual salary of $65,000
  • Comprehensive employee benefits
  • Fully remote work arrangement
  • Opportunity to collaborate with national environmental justice leaders
  • Professional development opportunities
  • Experience working within a major national coalition

Key Responsibilities

The Policy & Advocacy Fellow will contribute across three major areas:

1. Campaign Strategy and Networked Action

The successful candidate will:

  • Analyze advocacy campaigns led by A2 member organizations.
  • Identify opportunities for coordinated action across multiple states.
  • Develop practical campaign strategies that can be adapted by organizations with varying levels of capacity.
  • Produce advocacy resources including:
    • Model ordinances
    • Sample legislation
    • Campaign planning guides
    • Messaging frameworks
    • Advocacy toolkits
    • Talking points
  • Coordinate multi-state advocacy campaigns.
  • Organize national and regional “Days of Action.”
  • Track campaign outcomes and policy achievements.

2. Member Training and Capacity Building

Responsibilities include:

  • Leading policy and advocacy trainings.
  • Coaching grassroots organizations.
  • Building members’ understanding of policy processes.
  • Supporting organizations engaging with:
    • Public Service Commissions
    • Zoning boards
    • Local government agencies
    • Regional authorities
  • Helping members prepare:
    • Public comments
    • Testimony
    • Advocacy actions

It is important to note that this role does not involve direct lobbying activities.

3. Learning, Collaboration, and Documentation

The Fellow will also:

  • Facilitate peer-learning sessions among member organizations.
  • Represent Anthropocene Alliance within the SHIP coalition.
  • Share best practices across partner organizations.
  • Document successful advocacy approaches.
  • Monitor participation trends and identify opportunities for continuous improvement.
  • Strengthen collaboration across the national network.

Required Qualifications

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Minimum of two years of professional experience in policy, advocacy, community organizing, or related fields.
  • Experience in environmental justice, climate action, or social justice initiatives is strongly preferred.
  • Knowledge of local and state policy-making processes.
  • Ability to convert complex policy issues into practical advocacy resources.
  • Strong communication and presentation skills.
  • Experience facilitating workshops and training sessions.
  • Excellent organizational and project management abilities.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously.
  • Collaborative working style.
  • Self-motivation and accountability.
  • Passion for environmental justice and community empowerment.

Why Consider This Opportunity?

Working with Anthropocene Alliance offers numerous professional and personal benefits, including:

  • Contributing to meaningful environmental justice initiatives.
  • Supporting hundreds of grassroots organizations nationwide.
  • Working remotely from anywhere in the United States.
  • Collaborating with experts in climate policy and advocacy.
  • Building experience in national policy coordination.
  • Helping shape environmental policies affecting vulnerable communities.
  • Joining one of the country’s most influential environmental justice coalitions.

Employment Details

  • Position: Policy & Advocacy Fellow
  • Employment Type: Full-time (40 hours per week)
  • Work Location: Fully Remote
  • Eligible Location: Anywhere within the United States
  • Salary: $65,000 annually
  • Contract Duration: Two years (with potential for permanent employment)
  • Organization: Anthropocene Alliance (A2)

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit the following documents:

  • Updated Resume
  • Cover Letter

Applications should be emailed to:

Jobs@AnthropoceneAlliance.org

or visit the official website and apply

In your cover letter, be sure to mention where you learned about the position, as requested by the employer.

Applicants may also be asked to provide:

  • LinkedIn Profile URL
  • Current employer information
  • Earliest available start date

Application Deadline

No application deadline has been specified, so applicants are encouraged to apply early. 

Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible, as the position may be filled once a suitable candidate has been identified.

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Policy & Advocacy Fellow at Anthropocene Alliance (A2): Fully Remote U.S. Environmental Justice Job Paying $65,000 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
